compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Plan-review triage (round 5 PASS, signed; residual advisories judged non-actionable with justification per gate protocol):
- P6 (0.4, recurring identical finding id since round 1): DET shape heuristic on 'and'-bundled criterion lines. ACs were split across rounds 2-5; the remaining bundles are semantically atomic (one test with parametrized default/explicit-False variants; a dated note per ADR). Further splitting reduces readability without adding verifiability.
- T8 (0.0, rising_floor.py:86 section comment): already IN SCOPE of the plan as written — the What section makes the grep rule normative ('the enumeration is informative; the grep rule is normative, covering residuals') and this hit matches 'inert.*default'. It will be updated by the sweep.
- G6 (0.0, rationale for superseding the discriminates_novelty eval): recorded here — the eval guarded against a non-discriminating novelty signal causing wrongful drops; that risk is bounded because the floor is fail-safe (any reader/hash/novelty error -> zero drops), precision-first (T_novel 0.7; priority floor 0.4 = corpus p40 'below major'), drops ONLY novel+low-priority findings in the advisory bucket (never blocking findings), and has a trivial total back-out (explicit false). Meanwhile the measured cost of running un-floored is high (32% verdict instability on identical plans; 95% of remediation edits mint new findings; 60% of gate compute in the 4+-round tail — and this very ticket took 5 rounds for a 2-line config flip). The operator explicitly authorized default-ON on this evidence; the eval remains available to re-run before flag REMOVAL (ticket 49ae-258b-4534-4d83).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Field evidence since the 2026-07-08 plan-review recalibration (782 runs / 225 tickets): 18% of tickets need 4+ review rounds and the sticky tail consumes 60% of gate compute; 38 PASS->BLOCK flips (22 remediation-induced, 16 pure sampling-miss); 32% verdict instability on byte-identical plans; 95% of remediation edits mint new findings (net +324 across 430 edit rounds). The shipped cross-round convergence machinery — the plan-review rising floor (epic 7d43, children ec89/cc5b) and the code-review region-gated floor (ADR 0037) — is INERT behind default-False flags. The operator has authorized flipping both flags ON BY DEFAULT on this field evidence (superseding the discriminates_novelty-eval prerequisite), with a follow-up task (49ae-258b-4534-4d83) to remove the flags entirely.

## What
- In src/rebar/_config_schema.py flip `remediation_mode: bool = False` -> `True` (line 264). Flip `novelty_drop_active: bool = False` -> `True` (line 282).
- SHARED GATE — intentional cross-gate effect: `novelty_drop_active` is consumed by BOTH plan-review (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:204) AND the code-review region-gated floor (src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608 via src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:720). Flipping it by default activates the code-review floor too; this is deliberate, included in the operator authorization, verified by a dedicated AC below.
- RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP (the authoritative definition of doc/comment scope): rerun `grep -rniE 'off by default|default[- ]off|defaults?_off|default off|inert.*default|default.*inert|flag is False' src/ tests/ docs/` at implementation time (note `defaults?_off` also catches underscore spellings inside Python identifiers) plus a review of test-function NAMES in the four floor/gate test modules, updating EVERY hit that refers to `remediation_mode`, `novelty_drop_active`, the plan-review rising floor, or the code-review region-gated floor to default-ON framing naming explicit `false` as the back-out. Known hits as of HEAD (informative; the grep+names rule is normative, covering residuals):
  - src/rebar/_config_schema.py:259-263 + 278-281 (the two flag comments, incl. 'Default OFF for v1 (expand-contract…)' and 'The EVIDENCE GATE: … done MANUALLY by the operator only after 150b's discriminates_novelty eval…')
  - src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:81 ('default-OFF v1 rollout'), :188-195 (`_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring), :~523 ('inert (and the verdict byte-identical) by default')
  - src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:597 ('OFF by default') + :608 ('(off by default)')
  - tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:7 module docstring ('the default-OFF config rollout'), its section comments, and the stale test NAME `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` at :190 (rename to `test_config_defaults_on_and_60_minutes`, updating its body to the new True defaults)
  - tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 trailing inline comment ('# inert by default (the evidence gate)')
  - docs/config.md:107-118, docs/plan-review-gate.md:322-338 + 370-374, docs/review-kernel.md:124
  - docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:71, docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:55 + 88-96, docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md:92 + 167 — for ADRs add a dated 'defaults flipped ON (operator-authorized on field evidence, 2026-07-11)' note rather than rewriting ADR history.
- NON-GOALS: completion_floor_active (epic 66ac, separate evidence gate) stays False; novelty_drop_threshold (0.7) / novelty_priority_floor (0.4) / remediation_window_minutes (60) unchanged; no change to the floor math or eligibility logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VerifyConfig.remediation_mode defaults to True in src/rebar/_config_schema.py:264.
- [ ] VerifyConfig.novelty_drop_active defaults to True in src/rebar/_config_schema.py:282.
- [ ] A test constructs a default VerifyConfig() plus explicit-False variants, proving default-ON activates each floor's config gate while explicit false restores the byte-identical full-review off-path.
- [ ] A test covers the cross-gate side-effect: with a default (unmodified) config, the code-review region-gated floor's evidence-gate check (src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:608) proceeds rather than short-circuiting.
- [ ] tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 asserts `vc.novelty_drop_active is True`.
- [ ] tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192 asserts `vc.remediation_mode is True`.
- [ ] `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` is renamed per the sweep rule.
- [ ] The rule-based sweep grep (as defined in What) returns zero hits that still claim default-OFF/inert-by-default for the two flags or their floors, across src/ + tests/ + docs/.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md records that the flip was operator-authorized on field evidence in lieu of the discriminates_novelty eval.
- [ ] Each of docs/adr/0002, docs/adr/0008, docs/adr/0037 carries a dated defaults-flipped note.
- [ ] completion_floor_active remains default False.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
compiled_state.title: Convergence floors on by default: flip remediation_mode + novelty_drop_active defaults to True

verify_commands.command: make lint && make typecheck && make test
python -c "from rebar._config_schema import VerifyConfig; c=VerifyConfig(); assert c.remediation_mode and c.novelty_drop_active and not c.completion_floor_active"
